posts.linkedin:
You applied to 50 jobs.

You have 8 years of experience. Strong reviews. Real results.

You got 2 interviews.

And at least one of those came from a referral — not from your resume.

You're not underqualified. You're being filtered before a single human sees your application.

75% of qualified candidates are rejected by Applicant Tracking Systems before a recruiter reads their resume. These systems — Workday, Greenhouse, Lever, Taleo — don't read your resume the way a person does. They run a scoring algorithm against the job description. If your language doesn't match their language, you're out. Automatically. Regardless of your actual ability.

This is not a talent problem. It's an alignment problem.

We built a free guide around the exact system to fix it: the P.A.S.S.E.R.™ Framework — 6 phases to pass ATS filters, increase interview callbacks by 2–5×, and receive offers within 30 days.

👉 Download free: https://publixion.com/guides/resume-rejections-despite-experience

#JobSearch #ResumeWriting #CareerAdvice #ATSTips #Publixion

posts.linkedin:
Most experienced professionals have the same resume problem.

They describe what they did. The job description describes what the company needs. Those two languages rarely match — and ATS systems only score matches.

Here's the 6-phase framework from our free resume guide:

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
THE P.A.S.S.E.R.™ FRAMEWORK
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

P — Position Deconstruction
Copy every job description before applying. Highlight required skills, tools, certifications, repeated phrases. Any term appearing 3+ times is a priority keyword — it must appear in your resume at least twice.

A — Alignment Mapping
Build a two-column table: JD requirement on the left, your matching experience on the right. Gaps must be addressed before applying. If you can't match a requirement — reframe adjacent experience or address it in the cover letter.

S — Structure Optimization
Strip all ATS-breaking formatting. No tables. No columns. No text boxes. No graphics. Standard headings only: Summary, Experience, Skills, Education, Certifications. Save as .docx for ATS upload.

S — Strength Amplification (A.R.I. Formula™)
Every bullet: Action Verb + Result + Impact. Every bullet answers: How much? How fast? How often? Compared to what?

Before: "Managed team projects."
After: "Led cross-functional team of 6 to deliver product launch 2 weeks early, generating $120K in Q1 revenue."

E — Evaluation Testing
Run your resume through Jobscan, ResumeWorded, or SkillSyncer against the specific JD. Target: 75%+ match score. Do not apply below 70%.

R — Refinement Loop
Never send the same resume to two different roles. Each application gets its own version. Track versions, callback rates, and ATS scores in the Interview Conversion Tracker (included in the guide).

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Case study: A senior marketing manager went from 3 callbacks in 60 applications to 9 callbacks in 22 applications — after applying P.A.S.S.E.R.™. Average ATS score: 41% → 83%. Two offers in 30 days.

👉 Full guide, free: https://publixion.com/guides/resume-rejections-despite-experience

#ResumeWriting #JobSearch #CareerAdvice #ATSTips #Publixion

posts.linkedin:
The P.A.S.S.E.R.™ resume guide is for a very specific professional.

Not a recent graduate. This person:

→ Has 3–15 years of experience and is not getting the interview rate their background should produce
→ Has been in the job market for more than 60 days with fewer than 3 callbacks from direct applications
→ Has lost at least one role to a candidate they believe was less qualified
→ Sends the same resume (or a lightly edited version) to every application
→ Has never run their resume through an ATS testing tool
→ Uses a resume template with columns, icons, or a designed header — and doesn't know that this is causing parsing failures
→ Writes experience bullets that start with "Responsible for" or "Assisted with"
→ Is preparing for a job search in the next 30 days and wants to enter it with a fully optimized document

If 3 or more of those are true — your resume is the bottleneck. The P.A.S.S.E.R.™ guide gives you the system to fix it.

Free. No email required.

👉 https://publixion.com/guides/resume-rejections-despite-experience

#JobSearch #ResumeWriting #CareerAdvice #ProfessionalDevelopment #Publixion

posts.linkedin:
Let me walk you through exactly how the P.A.S.S.E.R.™ framework gets applied in one week.

Meet Aisha. Operations Director. 14 years of experience. Former VP-level title at her last company.

6 months of job searching. Almost no callbacks.

Her resume was well-formatted, detailed, and impressive to a human reader. Her average ATS score: 38%. Her average callback rate: under 3%.

Root problem: Her resume used internal company terminology — "Process Excellence Lead" — instead of the industry-standard title recruiters and ATS systems search for.

She reads the guide on a Sunday.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SUNDAY — Keyword extraction
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
She runs the P step on 5 target JDs. The phrase "Director of Operations" appears in all 5. Her resume said "Head of Operational Excellence." Different enough to score near zero for the title match.

She also identifies: "cross-functional collaboration," "P&L ownership," "vendor management," and "KPI reporting" — all present in the JDs, all absent from her resume.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
MONDAY — Alignment mapping and rewrite
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
She rebuilds her summary using the guide's template. Retitles her previous role to match market-standard language. Rewrites 8 bullets using the A.R.I. formula.

Before: "Led efficiency improvement initiatives across operations."
After: "Directed $2.1M annual cost reduction program across 4 departments through cross-functional process redesign."

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
TUESDAY — ATS testing
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Jobscan score: 41% → 79%. She applies to the 3 roles with the highest alignment scores.

All 3 progress past ATS. Two offers received.
Accepted: Director of Operations. $165,000. 22% above previous compensation.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

6 months of searching ended in 45 days. The experience was always there. The alignment wasn't.

👉 Read the guide: https://publixion.com/guides/resume-rejections-despite-experience

#CareerAdvice #JobSearch #ResumeWriting #OperationsManagement #Publixion
posts.facebook:
Here's a real example of how the P.A.S.S.E.R.™ resume framework gets applied.

Imagine you're a data analyst with 6 years of experience. You've applied to 30 positions this quarter. Interview rate: 5%.

Your resume is accurate. Well-written. Clean design.

But your ATS score averages 43% — and you've never measured it.

You read the guide on a Tuesday evening.

📌 STEP 1 — Keyword extraction (P)
You run the extraction protocol on 3 target JDs. SQL appears 5 times. Tableau 3 times. KPI dashboards twice. Power BI twice.

Your resume mentions all of these — but in different phrasing. "Data visualization tools" instead of "Tableau." "Reporting systems" instead of "KPI dashboards."

📌 STEP 2 — Alignment mapping (A)
You replace every synonym with the exact JD language. Each tool is now named explicitly: SQL, Tableau, Power BI.

📌 STEP 3 — Bullet rewrite (S)
Before: "Worked with data to support leadership decisions."
After: "Built SQL-powered KPI dashboards in Tableau, reducing executive reporting cycle from 5 days to same-day delivery."

📌 STEP 4 — ATS test (E)
Jobscan score: 43% → 81%. You apply to 5 highly aligned roles.

Interview rate: 5% → 28%. Two offers in 3 weeks.

📖 Read free: https://publixion.com/guides/resume-rejections-despite-experience

#DataAnalyst #JobSearch #ResumeWriting #Publixion

posts.linkedin:
"My resume is already good. I don't think this is the problem."

That's the most common thing I hear from professionals who are getting rejected. Let me work through it.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
❌ "My resume looks professional — it can't be the issue."
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
A resume that looks professional to a human can be completely unreadable to an ATS. Tables, columns, and graphics — all common in professionally designed templates — cause ATS parsers to skip your skills section entirely. The content might be excellent. The system never sees it.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
❌ "I've tailored my resume before — it didn't help."
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Most tailoring means changing a few words in the summary. The P.A.S.S.E.R.™ system requires extracting 15–20 keywords from the JD, mapping them to your experience, and confirming your ATS score is above 70% before applying. That level of specificity is different from a light edit.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
❌ "The job market is just bad right now."
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
It may be. But the case studies in the guide — all in the current market — show professionals with 6–14 years of experience increasing their callback rates by 3–5× within 30 days. The market hasn't changed. Their ATS alignment scores have.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
❌ "This takes too much time for every application."
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
The keyword extraction takes 15 minutes. The mapping takes 15 minutes. The ATS test takes 5 minutes. 35 minutes per application — to avoid being filtered by a system that takes 35 seconds to reject you.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
❌ "Is the guide free?"
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Free. No email required.

👉 https://publixion.com/guides/resume-rejections-despite-experience

#ResumeWriting #JobSearch #CareerAdvice #ATSTips #Publixion

The most common objections to fixing your resume — addressed:

❓ "My resume looks professional."
Professional design breaks ATS parsers. Tables, columns, and graphics cause systems to skip your skills section entirely. Beautiful to humans. Invisible to machines.

❓ "I've tailored my resume before."
Changing a few summary words is not the P.A.S.S.E.R.™ system. The guide requires extracting 15–20 JD keywords, mapping them, and confirming 70%+ ATS score before applying.

❓ "The job market is just bad."
The case studies in this guide were conducted in the current market. Callback rates improved 3–5× within 30 days of applying the framework.

❓ "Too much work per application."
